2603.19146.title: D5P4: Partition Determinantal Point Process for Diversity in Parallel Discrete Diffusion Decoding
2603.19146.paper_summary: Discrete diffusion models are promising alternatives to autoregressive approaches for text generation, yet their decoding methods remain under-studied. Standard decoding methods for autoregressive models, such as beam search, do not directly apply to iterative denoising, and existing diffusion decoding techniques provide limited control over in-batch diversity. To bridge this gap, we introduce a generalized beam-search framework for discrete diffusion that generates candidates in parallel and supports modular beam-selection objectives. As a diversity-focused instantiation, we propose D5P4, which formulates the selection step as MAP inference over a Determinantal Point Process. Leveraging a scalable greedy solver, D5P4 maintains multi-GPU compatibility and enables an explicit trade-off between model probability and target diversity with near-zero compute overhead. Experiments on free-form generation and question answering demonstrate that D5P4 improves diversity over strong baselines while maintaining competitive generation quality.
2603.19146.paper_summary_zh: 離散擴散模型是文本生成中自回歸方法的有希望的替代方案，但其解碼方法仍然未受到充分研究。自回歸模型的標準解碼方法，如束搜索，無法直接應用於迭代去噪，而現有的擴散解碼技術對批次內的多樣性控制有限。為了填補這一空白，我們引入了一個通用的離散擴散束搜索框架，該框架能夠並行生成候選者並支持模塊化的束選擇目標。作為一種以多樣性為重點的實現，我們提出了D5P4，將選擇步驟公式化為對行列式點過程的MAP推斷。利用可擴展的貪婪求解器，D5P4保持了多GPU兼容性，並在模型概率和目標多樣性之間實現了明確的權衡，幾乎沒有計算開銷。在自由形式生成和問題回答的實驗中，D5P4在保持競爭性生成質量的同時，提高了多樣性，超越了強基準。
2603.19146.author: Jonathan Lys et.al.
2603.19146.authors: Jonathan Lys, Vincent Gripon, Bastien Pasdeloup, Axel Marmoret, Lukas Mauch, Fabien Cardinaux, Ghouthi Boukli Hacene
